role_delineation_check: Social Worker (SW) is responsible for identifying and scheduling the medical transport vendor for the stretcher discharge. The Patient Navigator (PN) is responsible for auditing Atlantis demographics, educating the family on the Caller ID V-Card, and flagging transport delays to the SW while maintaining the 'Wait' default.
demographic_audit_note: Audit of Atlantis queue against facility Face Sheet revealed a mismatch in the primary phone number (missing one digit) and an outdated Medicare Advantage plan ID; flagged both to SW for correction to ensure HHA referral accuracy.
weekly_staff_update: Week 2: SW Sarah confirms patient is meeting PT goals for bilateral TKA; HHA referral accepted by 'Silver Star Home Health' for 10/25 start-of-care.
v_card_flyer_status: V-Card successfully inserted into Mr. Bennett's mobile device for Caller ID recognition; program flyer delivered to daughter/POA during bedside visit.
pre_dc_pulse_call: 24-hour pulse call conducted; daughter expresses high anxiety (Sentiment Score: Anxious) because the facility has not provided a confirmed pickup window for the stretcher transport.
atlantis_final_sync: Discharge date of 10/24 and first home visit date of 10/25 successfully entered into Atlantis portal.
ma_visit_booking: MA visit scheduled for 10/25 at 11:00 AM, confirmed only after SW Sarah provided the HHA nurse's name and start-of-care window.
narrative_summary: Audited patient data in Atlantis to resolve a phone number discrepancy that would have blocked HHA intake. Educated the family on recognizing the Healing Partners Caller ID to ensure high engagement for the post-discharge pulse call. When the stretcher transport failed to arrive on the discharge date, documented the delay in Atlantis and flagged the barrier to the Social Worker, adhering to the 'Wait' default until the transition was re-secured.

format_1_state_log.event_description: Atlantis Data Lag: Phone number in system is missing a digit compared to Face Sheet.
Sentiment_Readiness_Gap: Daughter reports high anxiety regarding managing bilateral TKA recovery at home.
Transport Coordination Failure: Stretcher van is a no-show for the 10:00 AM discharge window.

format_2_triples.situation: Patient's phone number in Atlantis is incorrect, preventing HHA from reaching the family.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Flagged the demographic error to the SW and verified the correct number from the Face Sheet.

format_2_triples.situation: Family is overwhelmed by the logistics of a bilateral knee recovery.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Educated the daughter on what to expect during the first 24 hours home, including the MA arrival time.

format_2_triples.situation: Facility staff reports the transport company is not answering their calls.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Documented the transport barrier in Atlantis and asked the SW for an updated discharge timeline.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Document the transport no-show in the Atlantis case notes and wait for the Social Worker to provide a new discharge date.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Respects the SW boundary and the 'Wait' default, ensuring the PN does not overstep into vendor management.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Verify the family's current sentiment after the delay, educate them on why the transport is being rescheduled by the SW, and flag the new timeline to the MA team.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Focuses on family support and internal data synchronization without contacting the transport company directly.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Call the transport vendor directly to investigate the delay and negotiate a priority pickup for Mr. Bennett to ensure the MA visit is not missed.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Directly violates the No-Vendor rule and uses 'Fixer' verbs (Investigate, Negotiate), which is an automatic overstep.
